name: Fallen Chocolate Souffle Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 oz Bittersweet chocolate or semisweet chocolate, chopped fine
1/2 cup Unsweetened Dutch process cocoa powder
recipeInstructions:
Equipment: 8-inch round springform pan or 2-3 inch deep dish. Optional: 8 cardboard cake circles.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Place rack in the bottom third of the oven.
Line the bottom of the pan with parchment paper (unless serving directly from the pan).
Spray the sides with vegetable oil spray.
In a food processor or blender, pulverize almonds and flour until very fine. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine chopped chocolate, cocoa, and 3/4 cup sugar.
Add boiling water and whisk until smooth and chocolate is fully melted.
Whisk in egg yolks and brandy. Set aside.
In a medium mixing bowl, beat egg whites and cream of tartar on medium speed until soft peaks form.
Gradually add remaining powdered sugar and beat on high speed until stiff but not dry.
Fold the flour and almond mixture into the chocolate mixture.
Gently fold about one quarter of the egg whites into the chocolate mixture to lighten it.
Fold in the remaining egg whites.
Pour batter into prepared pan and level the top if needed.
Bake for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out with moist crumbs clinging to it.
Cool in the pan on a wire rack. The torte will sink like a souffle; the top may be uneven.
To release the cake, carefully slide a knife between the torte and the pan, running it around the pan.
Invert the cake onto a serving plate or cardboard circle.
Turn right side up and dust with powdered sugar before serving.
Yield: 10 servings
Chef: Alice Medrich
Show: #DJ9150. TV Food Network
